articles.title: Rain brings relief from heat wave
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo) Jammu and Kashmir
articles.full_text:
Rain brings relief from heat wave
Excelsior Correspondent JAMMU, June 16: Heavy to moderate rain during morning hours in Jammu and its adjoining areas today brought great relief from the heat wave while gusty winds swept Srinagar and some parts of Kashmir on Tuesday, causing damage...
Excelsior Correspondent
JAMMU, June 16: Heavy to moderate rain during morning hours in Jammu and its adjoining areas today brought great relief from the heat wave while gusty winds swept Srinagar and some parts of Kashmir on Tuesday, causing damage to a few structures, as rains lashed many parts of the valley.
Reports said high-speed winds caused damage to residential houses and shops in Sopore and other areas in north Kashmir's Baramulla district. However, there were no reports of any loss of life or injuries in these incidents, according to officials.
The police evacuated the famous Dal Lake of tourists in time as the strong winds swept the water body. No untoward incident was reported from the Lake.
Rains lashed the summer capital of the Union Territory and many other areas of the valley.
The rainfall, with brief but intense showers at some places, was recorded in Kupwara, Baramulla, Bandipora, Ganderbal, Budgam and parts of South Kashmir. The rainfall was accompanied by thunderstorms and lightning.
Meanwhile, heavy to moderate rain lashed winter capital Jammu, parts of Samba, Reasi, Doda, Ramban and Udhampur districts today, providing great relief to the people from heat wave. There was significant fall in temperature in the evening. Even during late evening, light rain was experienced in several areas of Jammu. The waterlogging was witnessed on many city roads and crossings during morning hours.
Winter capital Jammu recorded a maximum temperature of 32.6 degrees Celsius and minimum 20.3 degrees; Katra town a day temperature of 32.0 deg C and night temperature of 21.2 deg; Bhaderwah maximum 26.4 deg and minimum 12.8 degree C; Batote town maximum 26.8 and minimum 15.8 while Banihal registered day temperature of 26.3 deg C today.
Samba town recorded a day temperature of 41.6 deg C, Kathua 38.5 deg C, Udhampur 34.2 deg , Rajouri 31.6 deg, Ramban 40.5 while Kishtwar 27.8 deg C today. Jammu recorded 54 mm rainfall during past 24 hours, followed by 19.2 mm in Pahalgam. Banihal recorded 3.2 mm, Samba 21 mm and so on.
The Met office has predicted a spell of light to moderate rain/thundershower/ lightning at many places in Jammu and Kashmir during next 24 hours. The intensity may be high in south Kashmir and hilly areas of the region.
